text.title: Linear Issue Tracking (PM Agile) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Linear Issue Tracking (PM Agile). Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: One workspace per organisation; one team per actual team (not per project). Multiple workspaces = sync hell.
rule.rationale: Linear's design assumes one workspace; cross-workspace ops are intentionally limited.

rule.statement: Fixed cycle cadence: 1 week or 2 weeks. Mixed cadences across teams break velocity comparison.
rule.rationale: Velocity is cycle-bound; mixed cadence forces re-normalisation that erases the signal.

rule.statement: States: Backlog → Todo → In Progress → In Review → Done → Cancelled. ≤7 states total; no per-team state proliferation.
rule.rationale: State proliferation kills filter consistency + reports; ≤7 keeps mental model intact.

rule.statement: ≤15 labels per workspace; labels are types not statuses (bug / feat / chore / spike / docs). State is a separate axis.
rule.rationale: Label sprawl is the leading cause of Linear workspace bankruptcy in year 1.

rule.statement: GitHub (PR linkage), Slack (notifications), and Calendar (cycle reminders) integrations turned on. One-way push = invisible work.
rule.rationale: Bidirectional sync is Linear's value over GitHub Issues; not using it = downgrade.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-linear-issue-tracking.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
